Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Propane or Natural Gas Island Grill – 864 sq. in. Cooking, 48,000 BTU Main + 12,000 BTU Side Burner, Porcelain Grates, Storage Cabinets, Semi-Portable - GM/Ash Stone

The Fresno is aimed squarely at serious entertainers who need maximum cooking real estate and the rare ability to relocate a grill island. With 864 sq. in. of cooking surface, a 48,000 BTU main burner array, a 12,000 BTU side burner, and an attractive GM/Ash Stone finish, it functions as a genuine outdoor kitchen centerpiece. The semi-portable design is unusual in this category and makes it practical for renters or anyone who doesn't want a permanently anchored structure. Porcelain-coated grates retain heat well and clean up more easily than bare cast iron.

At $1,399.99 it is the most expensive product in this lineup, and the 4.2/5 rating from only 16 reviews introduces more uncertainty than you'd like at that price point. The smaller review pool means edge-case quality or assembly issues may not yet be fully represented. Buyers should also note that "semi-portable" comes with practical limits — this is still a substantial unit, not something you wheel around casually. If portability isn't a priority, the broader gas grill category has options with deeper review histories at comparable specs.

Brand-Man Gas Grill Built-In Head, 30-Inch 4-Burner Propane Grill, Natural Gas Convertible, Heavy Duty 304 Stainless Steel 40,000BTUs BBQ Island Outdoor Kitchen

For anyone building a custom outdoor kitchen island, this drop-in grill head is the standout value in the built-in segment. The use of 304 stainless steel — a marine-grade alloy — means it holds up to weather and grease far better than lesser stainless grades, and 40,000 BTU across four burners is sufficient for high-heat searing and zone cooking simultaneously. A perfect 5/5 rating across 72 reviews is a strong signal; that sample size is large enough to carry real weight. Natural gas convertibility makes it a flexible fit for any island build regardless of fuel source.

As a head-only unit at $889.99, the total project cost rises quickly once you account for an island enclosure, which Brand-Man does not include here. This is not a criticism of the grill itself, but buyers comparing sticker prices against complete island stations should factor in full build costs. The 30-inch footprint also requires precise island planning — measure carefully before ordering, as built-in installations leave no room for error on dimensions.

Hygrill STD Series Heavy Duty 3-Burner Built In Grill, Stainless Steel Grill Head for Outdoor Kitchen, Natural Gas

The Hygrill STD Series is the pick for outdoor kitchen builders who prioritize build quality and long-term durability over maximizing BTU count or burner quantity. A 5/5 rating from 41 reviews is among the most credible scores in this roundup — enough responses to establish a pattern. "Heavy duty" in the product name is backed by its construction, making it appropriate for year-round use in variable climates. At $1,129.99 it sits at the premium end of built-in heads, reflecting genuine material and engineering investment rather than brand markup alone.

Three burners instead of four limits simultaneous cooking zones, which matters during large gatherings where you need to hold food at different temperatures across the grill surface. Natural gas only — there is no LPG conversion option noted — so propane users are excluded unless they're running a permanent gas line. If you're comparing built-in options and want more burners at a lower price point, the Brand-Man head above offers a direct alternative worth reviewing alongside the propane gas grill sale options for broader context.

Monument Grills Larger 4+2 Burner Propane Gas Grills Stainless Steel Cabinet Style with Clearview® Lid, Knob Controls, Built-In Thermometer, Infrared Side Sear Burners & Side Burners, Blue

At $519, the Monument Grills cabinet-style unit is the most accessible entry point in this group for buyers who want a full-featured freestanding grill without committing to a built-in installation. The 4+2 burner configuration — four main burners plus two infrared side sear burners and side burners — is unusually versatile for this price tier. The Clearview® lid with a built-in thermometer and dedicated sear burners address the two most common complaints about mid-range grills: guesswork on temperature and lack of high-heat searing capability. A 5/5 rating from 71 reviews is the strongest credibility combination in this lineup.

Cabinet-style freestanding grills invite more scrutiny on long-term weather resistance than built-in stainless units, and the blue finish, while distinctive, is a polarizing aesthetic choice that won't suit every patio. Propane-only operation also means no natural gas option for those with existing gas lines — if NG compatibility matters, see the gas grills on sale page for convertible alternatives. Buyers focused purely on cooking performance per dollar will find it hard to beat, but those building a cohesive outdoor kitchen aesthetic may want a different form factor.

Stanbroil 28-Inch Premium Built-In Natural Gas Griddle for Outdoor Kitchen Grill, Durable Stainless Steel 2-Burner Flat Top Grill with Lid for BBQ Island, 34,000 BTUs

The Stanbroil griddle occupies a different niche than every other product here: it is a flat-top cooking surface, not a grate grill. That distinction makes it the right choice for outdoor kitchen builders who want to expand their cooking repertoire to smash burgers, breakfast foods, stir-fry, and delicate proteins that fall through grill grates. At $1,109.99 for a 28-inch, 34,000 BTU natural gas built-in with a premium stainless construction and included lid, it is competitively priced against standalone flat-top units of similar quality. A 4.5/5 from 20 reviews reflects solid satisfaction, though the smaller sample size carries the usual caveats.

It is only a griddle — there is no grate option, so it cannot replace a conventional grill in a single-appliance setup. It is best understood as a complement to a grill head rather than a standalone solution. Natural gas only, with no propane conversion, limits flexibility. The 28-inch footprint also requires adequate island counter space adjacent to any existing grill head. Buyers exploring built-in griddle alternatives should cross-reference with the charcoal and gas hybrid options if they want maximum cooking versatility from a single unit.

Buying guide

BTU output sets the ceiling on how fast a grill recovers heat and how well it handles high-volume cooks. The products here range from 34,000 BTU (the Stanbroil 2-burner griddle) up to a combined 60,000 BTU on the Tytus Fresno (48,000 main burners plus a 12,000 BTU side burner). More BTUs matter most when you're cooking for large groups or searing at high temperatures — for everyday grilling, 40,000–44,000 BTU across four burners, as on both Brand-Man models, is a practical middle ground. Spreading output across more burners also gives you better zone control, which is more useful than raw total BTU in most real cooking scenarios.

Cooking surface and burner count directly determine how many people you can feed at once. The Tytus Fresno tops the lineup at 864 sq. in., and its 5-burner layout lets you run multiple temperature zones simultaneously. The Monument Grills 4+2 unit adds infrared side sear burners on top of two side burners, which broadens the type of cooking rather than just the quantity. If your primary use is flat-top cooking rather than grate grilling, the Stanbroil 28-inch griddle is purpose-built for that style with a dedicated flat stainless surface. Match surface type to cooking style before chasing square-inch numbers.

Build quality in this category comes down to steel grade and grate material. The Brand-Man built-in head specifies 304 stainless steel — a marine-grade alloy that resists rust and handles outdoor exposure far better than 430 stainless or painted steel. Porcelain-coated grates, as on the Tytus Fresno, retain heat well and resist sticking, but require careful handling to avoid chipping. For island builds specifically, the Hygrill STD Series and both Brand-Man units are designed as built-in heads, meaning they drop into a surround rather than sitting on a freestanding cart — that distinction matters enormously if you're planning a permanent outdoor kitchen versus a movable setup.

Fuel flexibility is worth checking before you buy. Several units here — including the Brand-Man 4-burner island and the Brand-Man built-in head — are convertible between LPG and natural gas, which gives you the option to run a dedicated gas line later without replacing the grill. The Tytus Fresno is also listed as propane or natural gas compatible. If you're locked into a single fuel source, that flexibility has no value, but if there's any chance you'll add a gas line, a convertible model protects that investment. For broader context on fuel type and pricing across categories, see the best propane gas grills on sale or explore the full gas grills for sale guide to compare segments side by side.

Frequently asked questions

What is the price range for heavy-duty gas grills at Lowe's?

Among the models covered here, prices run from $519 for the Monument Grills 4+2 Burner cabinet-style grill up to $1,399.99 for the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island Grill. Built-in grill heads and griddles cluster in the $889–$1,130 range, while full outdoor kitchen island setups with storage cabinets typically sit between $800 and $1,400. If budget is the primary driver, the Monument at $519 delivers solid BTU output and infrared sear burners at the lowest entry point in this tier. For a full outdoor kitchen station, the Brand-Man 4-Burner Island at $799.99 is the most competitive option. For broader sale pricing across brands, see Best Gas Grills On Sale (2026).

Which brands are represented in Lowe's heavy-duty gas grill lineup?

The standout brands in this segment are Brand-Man, Tytus, Hygrill, Monument Grills, and Stanbroil. Brand-Man offers both a full outdoor kitchen island and a standalone built-in grill head, covering a wide range of installation scenarios. Hygrill and Stanbroil focus on built-in heads and griddles engineered for permanent outdoor kitchen setups. Monument Grills takes a freestanding cabinet approach with features like a Clearview lid and infrared side sear burners. Tytus positions its Fresno island as a semi-portable premium option with the highest BTU output in the group at 48,000 BTU main burners plus a 12,000 BTU side burner.

What is the difference between a built-in grill head and an outdoor kitchen island grill?

A built-in grill head — like the Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner or the Hygrill STD Series — is the cooking unit only, designed to drop into a custom masonry or prefabricated counter. You supply the surrounding structure. An outdoor kitchen island grill — like the Brand-Man 4-Burner Island or the Tytus Fresno — arrives as a self-contained unit with storage cabinets, side burners, and prep surfaces already integrated. Island grills are faster to set up and need no additional construction, but built-in heads allow more flexibility in counter dimensions and materials. If you are building a permanent outdoor kitchen from scratch, a grill head often makes more sense. If you want a turnkey station, an island is the practical choice.

Can these grills run on both propane and natural gas?

Most models in this group are dual-fuel convertible. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Island explicitly supports LPG/NG conversion, and the Brand-Man Built-In Head is listed as natural gas convertible. The Tytus Fresno is sold as propane or natural gas compatible. The Hygrill STD Series is listed specifically for natural gas. The Stanbroil griddle is also natural gas. Always verify the conversion kit is included or available separately before purchase — not all manufacturers include the hardware in the box. If propane is your primary fuel and you want sale pricing options, check Best Propane Gas Grills Sale (2026).

What BTU output should a heavy-duty outdoor gas grill have?

For serious outdoor cooking, 40,000 BTU or more across the main burners is a reasonable benchmark for grills in this class. The Brand-Man Built-In Head delivers 40,000 BTU, the Brand-Man Island puts out 44,000 BTU, and the Tytus Fresno leads at 48,000 BTU main plus a 12,000 BTU side burner. The Stanbroil griddle offers 34,000 BTU across two burners, which is appropriate for flat-top cooking. Raw BTU numbers matter less than BTU-per-square-inch of cooking surface — a grill with 864 sq. in. of cooking area like the Tytus needs more total output to reach the same surface temperature as a smaller unit. Look at both figures together when comparing models.

Are these grills suitable for permanent outdoor kitchen installations?

Several of the models here are engineered specifically for permanent installation. The Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head, the Hygrill STD Series, and the Stanbroil 28-Inch Griddle are all designed as drop-in units for BBQ islands and outdoor kitchen counters. The Tytus Fresno is described as semi-portable, meaning it can be repositioned if needed rather than being fully fixed. The freestanding Monument Grills cabinet-style unit is not a built-in product and is not intended for counter installation. If you are building a full outdoor kitchen and want to explore the broader category, Gas Grills For Sale — Best Picks, Reviews & Guides covers additional options across styles and price points.

Gas grills for sale span a wide range — from freestanding cabinet-style units built around propane tanks to fully integrated built-in grill heads designed for permanent outdoor kitchen islands. Buyers shopping this category generally fall into two camps: those outfitting a backyard kitchen from scratch who need a complete grill station with storage, side burners, and prep space, and those replacing or upgrading a built-in head within an existing structure. The main decision dimensions are fuel type (propane vs. natural gas, with many units offering dual-fuel convertibility), burner count and total BTU output, cooking surface area, and build quality — particularly whether the grill uses 304-grade stainless steel, which resists corrosion far better than lower-grade alternatives. Browse the current gas grill sale picks or narrow by type using the guides for propane grills on sale, charcoal-gas combos, and discounted gas grills to find the right fit for your setup and budget.

Subcategories

Propane Gas Grills

Propane grills dominate the heavy-duty outdoor market because tanks are portable, refillable anywhere, and deliver consistent pressure regardless of season. These pages cover freestanding and built-in propane models across a range of BTU outputs and cooking surface sizes, from compact backyard setups to full island configurations. Whether you're hunting a discounted unit or a current-season release, the propane-focused guides break down what's worth buying right now — see Best Propane Gas Grills Sale and Best Propane Gas Grills On Sale for the full breakdowns.

Charcoal and Hybrid Gas Grills

Some outdoor cooks want the convenience of gas ignition alongside the flavor profile that only charcoal or high-radiant heat can produce. This subcategory covers dual-fuel and charcoal-compatible grill designs that blur the line between traditional barbecue and modern gas cooking — suited to buyers who won't compromise on char. For fuel-specific options, see the guides on propane gas grills on sale and charcoal gas grills on sale, or browse the broader gas grills on sale roundup for additional picks.

Common questions in this category

What's the difference between a built-in grill head and a freestanding gas grill?

A built-in grill head drops into a custom outdoor kitchen frame or BBQ island, giving a seamless look and permanent installation — units like the Hygrill STD Series and Brand-Man 4-Burner Built-In Head are designed exactly this way. Freestanding grills come on their own cart or cabinet and can be repositioned or moved off a patio entirely. The right choice depends on whether you're building a fixed outdoor kitchen or want flexibility down the road. check this comparison.

For a deeper breakdown of built-in and freestanding options currently discounted, see Best Gas Grills On Sale (2026).

How much BTU output do you actually need for a heavy-duty outdoor grill?

BTU ratings indicate heat potential, but raw numbers only matter relative to cooking surface area — a grill with 40,000 BTUs across four burners gives roughly 10,000 BTUs per burner, which is a solid benchmark for searing. Going significantly lower risks uneven heat recovery during high-volume cooks; going much higher without the grate quality to match wastes fuel. Most heavy-duty propane grills in this category range from 34,000 to 48,000 BTUs total, which covers everything from weeknight dinners to large gatherings.

See current propane models with strong BTU-to-burner ratios in the Best Propane Gas Grills Sale (2026) guide.

Can most gas grills convert between propane and natural gas?

Many modern heavy-duty grills are sold as LPG/NG convertible, meaning they ship configured for one fuel type but include — or support purchasing — a conversion kit to switch to the other. This matters because natural gas requires a permanent line connection while propane uses portable tanks, and your setup at home will dictate which is practical. Always verify that conversion hardware is included or available before buying if you anticipate switching fuel sources later.

Explore propane-specific options currently on discount at Best Propane Gas Grills On Sale (2026).

Frequently asked questions

What is the difference between a built-in grill head and a freestanding gas grill?

A built-in grill head is designed to drop into or mount within an outdoor kitchen island or countertop frame — it has no legs or cart of its own. Models like the Hygrill STD Series Heavy Duty 3-Burner Built In Grill and the Brand-Man Gas Grill Built-In Head fall into this category. A freestanding grill sits on its own cabinet or cart frame. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island and the Monument Grills 4+2 Burner Propane Gas Grill are examples of self-contained units with integrated storage and prep space. If you already have or plan to build an outdoor kitchen frame, a built-in head gives a cleaner, permanent look. If you want a complete, movable cooking station without construction, a freestanding island grill is the faster path.

Propane vs. natural gas: which fuel type should you choose?

Natural gas connects directly to a home gas line, eliminating tank refills — ideal for a permanent outdoor kitchen setup. Propane (LPG) is stored in tanks, giving you flexibility to position the grill anywhere on a patio or deck without running a gas line. Many heavy-duty grills in this category are dual-fuel convertible, meaning they ship set up for one fuel but include hardware or conversion kits for the other. The Brand-Man Gas Grill Built-In Head is explicitly natural gas convertible, and the Brand-Man 4-Burner Grill Island is listed as LPG/NG convertible. If you are unsure which you will use long-term, prioritize a convertible model. For propane-specific deals, see Best Propane Gas Grills Sale (2026) and Best Propane Gas Grills On Sale (2026).

How many BTUs do you actually need for a heavy-duty outdoor grill?

BTU output matters most relative to cooking surface area, not as an isolated number. A high BTU rating on a small grate produces scorching heat with little control; too few BTUs on a large surface means uneven, slow cooking. The grills featured here range from 34,000 BTUs on the Stanbroil 28-Inch Built-In Natural Gas Griddle to 48,000 BTUs on the main burners of the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island Grill (plus a 12,000 BTU side burner). As a rough benchmark, aim for roughly 80–100 BTUs per square inch of primary cooking area for consistent searing performance at the heavy-duty level.

What does 304 stainless steel mean, and why does it matter?

304 stainless steel is a food-grade alloy containing roughly 18% chromium and 8% nickel, which gives it strong corrosion resistance and the ability to withstand repeated high-heat cycles without warping or rusting at the rate that lower-grade metals do. It is the industry standard for outdoor cooking equipment that is expected to last through years of weather exposure. The Brand-Man Gas Grill Built-In Head and the Brand-Man 4-Burner Grill Island both specify heavy-duty 304 stainless steel construction. When comparing grills, note whether the spec says "304" specifically — "stainless steel" alone can refer to lower grades like 430, which is more affordable but less rust-resistant in coastal or high-humidity environments.

What is the difference between a gas grill "on sale" and a gas grill "for sale"?

"For sale" simply means the product is currently available to purchase — no discount is implied. "On sale" specifically indicates a temporary price reduction from a higher regular price. This site covers both: Best Gas Grills On Sale (2026) and Best Gas Grills Sale (2026) focus on discounted pricing, while the broader hub covers the full range of available heavy-duty models regardless of whether a promotion is active. If your primary goal is capturing the lowest price on a specific model, check the on-sale pages first and use the hub pages to research which models are worth targeting before a sale event.

Should you consider a flat top griddle instead of a traditional grate grill?

A flat top griddle and a traditional grate grill serve different cooking styles. Grate grills deliver direct flame contact and the char marks associated with classic BBQ; a flat top creates a large, even cooking surface suited for smash burgers, eggs, stir-fry, seafood, and anything that would fall through grill grates. The Stanbroil 28-Inch Premium Built-In Natural Gas Griddle represents this category at the built-in level, rated 4.5/5 across 20 reviews. Some outdoor kitchen setups include both a grill head and a griddle side by side in the same island frame. If your cooking repertoire goes beyond steaks and chicken, a griddle module is worth factoring into the build from the start rather than retrofitting later. For charcoal alternatives in the same outdoor kitchen context, see Best Charcoal Gas Grills On Sale (2026).

What's the difference between a built-in grill head and a freestanding gas grill?

A built-in grill head drops into a custom outdoor kitchen frame or BBQ island, giving a seamless look and permanent installation — units like the Hygrill STD Series and Brand-Man 4-Burner Built-In Head are designed exactly this way. Freestanding grills come on their own cart or cabinet and can be repositioned or moved off a patio entirely. The right choice depends on whether you're building a fixed outdoor kitchen or want flexibility down the road. check this comparison.

For a deeper breakdown of built-in and freestanding options currently discounted, see Best Gas Grills On Sale (2026).

How much BTU output do you actually need for a heavy-duty outdoor grill?

Can most gas grills convert between propane and natural gas?

What is the difference between a built-in grill head and a freestanding gas grill?

A built-in grill head is designed to drop into or mount within an outdoor kitchen island or countertop frame — it has no legs or cart of its own. Models like the Hygrill STD Series Heavy Duty 3-Burner Built In Grill and the Brand-Man Gas Grill Built-In Head fall into this category. A freestanding grill sits on its own cabinet or cart frame. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island and the Monument Grills 4+2 Burner Propane Gas Grill are examples of self-contained units with integrated storage and prep space. If you already have or plan to build an outdoor kitchen frame, a built-in head gives a cleaner, permanent look. If you want a complete, movable cooking station without construction, a freestanding island grill is the faster path.

Propane vs. natural gas: which fuel type should you choose?

Natural gas connects directly to a home gas line, eliminating tank refills — ideal for a permanent outdoor kitchen setup. Propane (LPG) is stored in tanks, giving you flexibility to position the grill anywhere on a patio or deck without running a gas line. Many heavy-duty grills in this category are dual-fuel convertible, meaning they ship set up for one fuel but include hardware or conversion kits for the other. The Brand-Man Gas Grill Built-In Head is explicitly natural gas convertible, and the Brand-Man 4-Burner Grill Island is listed as LPG/NG convertible. If you are unsure which you will use long-term, prioritize a convertible model. For propane-specific deals, see Best Propane Gas Grills Sale (2026) and Best Propane Gas Grills On Sale (2026).

How many BTUs do you actually need for a heavy-duty outdoor grill?

BTU output matters most relative to cooking surface area, not as an isolated number. A high BTU rating on a small grate produces scorching heat with little control; too few BTUs on a large surface means uneven, slow cooking. The grills featured here range from 34,000 BTUs on the Stanbroil 28-Inch Built-In Natural Gas Griddle to 48,000 BTUs on the main burners of the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island Grill (plus a 12,000 BTU side burner). As a rough benchmark, aim for roughly 80–100 BTUs per square inch of primary cooking area for consistent searing performance at the heavy-duty level.

What does 304 stainless steel mean, and why does it matter?

304 stainless steel is a food-grade alloy containing roughly 18% chromium and 8% nickel, which gives it strong corrosion resistance and the ability to withstand repeated high-heat cycles without warping or rusting at the rate that lower-grade metals do. It is the industry standard for outdoor cooking equipment that is expected to last through years of weather exposure. The Brand-Man Gas Grill Built-In Head and the Brand-Man 4-Burner Grill Island both specify heavy-duty 304 stainless steel construction. When comparing grills, note whether the spec says "304" specifically — "stainless steel" alone can refer to lower grades like 430, which is more affordable but less rust-resistant in coastal or high-humidity environments.

"For sale" simply means the product is currently available to purchase — no discount is implied. "On sale" specifically indicates a temporary price reduction from a higher regular price. Should you consider a flat top griddle instead of a traditional grate grill?

A flat top griddle and a traditional grate grill serve different cooking styles. Grate grills deliver direct flame contact and the char marks associated with classic BBQ; a flat top creates a large, even cooking surface suited for smash burgers, eggs, stir-fry, seafood, and anything that would fall through grill grates. The Stanbroil 28-Inch Premium Built-In Natural Gas Griddle represents this category at the built-in level, rated 4.5/5 across 20 reviews. title: Natural Gas Outdoor Grills — Best Picks, Reviews & Guides
metaTitle: Natural Gas Outdoor Grills — Best Picks, Reviews & Guides
metaDescription: Natural Gas Outdoor Grills — Best Picks, Reviews & Guides. Editor's picks, in-depth review, and comparisons for "natural gas outdoor grills".
outline:
Disclosure: This page contains affiliate links. We may earn a commission if you purchase through them — at no extra cost to you. Recommendations are based on independent testing and editorial judgment.

Natural gas outdoor grills cover everything from freestanding cabinet-style units to built-in grill heads and complete island stations designed for permanent outdoor kitchens. Shoppers here range from homeowners running a gas line to an existing patio to serious backyard cooks building a full outdoor kitchen with storage, side burners, and integrated griddles. The core decision dimensions are configuration (built-in head vs. freestanding vs. full island), BTU output and burner count, cooking surface area, and whether the unit ships ready for natural gas or requires a propane-to-NG conversion kit. Material quality — particularly 304 stainless steel construction and porcelain or stainless grates — separates entry-level picks from heavy-duty options built to handle daily use and weather exposure year-round. For buyers focused on specific use cases, see the dedicated guides on best gas grills for outdoor kitchens, best barbeque grills in natural gas, and the broader best outdoor grills natural gas roundup.

Subcategories

Built-In Grills for Outdoor Kitchens

This cluster covers grill heads and built-in units designed to drop into a stone, concrete, or steel island frame — the kind of permanent setup where aesthetics and BTU output both matter. Products like the Hygrill STD Series Heavy Duty 3-Burner Built In Grill and the Brand-Man Gas Grill Built-In Head fall squarely here, offering stainless steel construction and direct natural gas hookups that eliminate the need for propane tanks. It's the right category if you're building or upgrading a fixed outdoor kitchen rather than buying a freestanding cart grill. Explore the best gas grills for outdoor kitchens.

Freestanding and Island Natural Gas Grills

For buyers who want a complete, self-contained grilling station — cabinets, side burners, prep tables, and all — this subcategory focuses on full island and cart-style natural gas grills. Units like the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island Grill and the Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island with Side Burner combine high BTU output with built-in storage, making them practical for frequent entertainers who don't want to integrate into masonry. Most models in this group are LPG/NG convertible, so they work whether your yard is plumbed for natural gas or not. See the top-rated outdoor natural gas grills.

BBQ and Barbecue Natural Gas Grills

This group targets traditional barbecue-style cooking on natural gas — think multi-burner setups with porcelain or stainless grates, high-capacity cooking surfaces, and classic cabinet styling. It overlaps with freestanding grills but skews toward buyers who prioritize low-and-slow BBQ performance alongside searing ability, rather than integrated kitchen functionality. Browse the best natural gas barbeque grills or check the closely related barbecue grills natural gas guide for additional picks and comparisons.

Featured products

The Hygrill STD Series Heavy Duty 3-Burner Built In Grill, Stainless Steel Grill Head for Outdoor Kitchen, Natural Gas earns a perfect 5/5 from 41 reviewers and is built specifically for drop-in outdoor kitchen installations, with a heavy-duty stainless steel head priced at $1,129.99. It's a strong pick for homeowners who want a clean, integrated look without sacrificing build quality.

The Brand-Man Gas Grill Built-In Head, 30-Inch 4-Burner Propane Grill, Natural Gas Convertible, Heavy Duty 304 Stainless Steel 40,000BTUs BBQ Island Outdoor Kitchen delivers 40,000 BTUs across four burners with 304-grade stainless steel construction, and at $889.99 it holds a perfect 5/5 rating across 72 reviews — the highest review count among the built-in heads here. Its natural gas convertibility adds long-term flexibility.

For buyers who want a complete outdoor cooking station rather than a grill head alone, the Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island with Side Burner - 44,000 BTU Stainless Steel BBQ Grill Station with Storage Cabinets, Fold-out Prep Table, LPG/NG Convertible, Black bundles 44,000 BTUs, a side burner, fold-out prep table, and enclosed storage cabinets into a single unit at $799.99, backed by 113 reviews averaging 4.7/5.

The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Propane or Natural Gas Island Grill is the highest-output option in this group, combining a 48,000 BTU main burner array with a 12,000 BTU side burner and 864 sq. in. of cooking space across porcelain grates — all in a semi-portable island format with storage cabinets at $1,399.99. It suits large households or anyone who regularly cooks for a crowd. For more complete outdoor kitchen setups, see the Best Gas Grills For Outdoor Kitchen guide.

The Stanbroil 28-Inch Premium Built-In Natural Gas Griddle for Outdoor Kitchen Grill, Durable Stainless Steel 2-Burner Flat Top Grill with Lid for BBQ Island, 34,000 BTUs fills a specific niche: a flat-top griddle surface rather than grate cooking, making it ideal for breakfast items, smash burgers, or seafood. Its 34,000 BTUs across two burners and lidded design are uncommon in built-in natural gas griddles at this $1,109.99 price point.

The Monument Grills Larger 4+2 Burner Propane Gas Grills Stainless Steel Cabinet Style with Clearview® Lid, Knob Controls, Built-In Thermometer, Infrared Side Sear Burners & Side Burners, Blue is the most affordable option at $519 and packs infrared side sear burners alongside two additional side burners — a feature set that typically appears on grills costing considerably more. Its Clearview® lid and 5/5 rating from 71 buyers make it a compelling freestanding alternative. Browse additional options in the Best Outdoor Grills Natural Gas and Best Barbeque Grills Natural Gas roundups.

Common questions in this category

Is a natural gas grill actually worth it over propane?

For anyone who cooks outdoors regularly, a direct natural gas connection eliminates the hassle of monitoring and swapping propane tanks — fuel is always on and the cost per BTU is typically lower than propane over time. The trade-off is that the grill must sit within reach of a gas line, so placement is fixed rather than flexible. If your outdoor space is permanent and you grill more than a few times a month, the convenience and long-term savings usually justify the conversion or upfront purchase.

See the top-rated natural gas outdoor grills →

What should you look for when buying a built-in grill for an outdoor kitchen?

Built-in grills need to fit a specific cabinet cutout, hold up to weather without a cover-friendly design, and be made from marine-grade or 304 stainless steel so they don't rust flush against surrounding stonework or cabinetry. BTU output, cooking area, and whether the unit ships ready for natural gas (or requires a conversion kit) are the other critical specs to confirm before ordering. A grill head rated for outdoor kitchen installation is not interchangeable with a freestanding unit — the ventilation, trim, and mounting requirements differ substantially.

Explore the best gas grills for outdoor kitchens →

How many BTUs do you actually need for outdoor grilling?

BTU ratings are useful for comparison but misleading in isolation — a well-designed 40,000 BTU grill with good heat retention will outperform a poorly built 60,000 BTU model. A practical rule of thumb is 80–100 BTUs per square inch of primary cooking area, which means a 400 sq. in. grill needs roughly 32,000–40,000 BTUs to sear and recover temperature quickly. Side burners, infrared sear zones, and griddle surfaces each carry separate BTU specs and should be evaluated independently from the main burner output.

Browse high-BTU natural gas barbecue grills →

Frequently asked questions

What is the difference between a built-in grill head and a freestanding natural gas grill?

A built-in grill head drops into a cutout in an outdoor kitchen island or counter, leaving only the cooking surface exposed. Models like the Hygrill STD Series and the Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head are designed specifically for this installation. Freestanding grills sit on their own cart or legs and can be repositioned. If you are planning a permanent outdoor kitchen, a built-in head gives a cleaner look and is generally easier to plumb to a natural gas line. If flexibility matters more than aesthetics, a freestanding or semi-portable option is the better fit.

Do I need a dedicated natural gas line, or can I convert a propane grill?

Most heavy-duty grills in this category are either factory-configured for natural gas or sold as convertible units. The Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head, for example, is listed as natural gas convertible, and the Brand-Man 4-Burner Grill Island is rated for both LPG and NG. Conversion typically requires a separate orifice kit and, in most municipalities, a licensed plumber or gas fitter to tap into the supply line. Converting without proper hardware voids warranties and creates a safety hazard, so verify the kit is included or available before purchasing a propane-primary model.

How do I choose between a grill head only and a full grill island station?

A grill head is the right buy when you already have an island structure or are building a custom outdoor kitchen with specific cabinet and countertop materials. A full island station, like the Brand-Man 4-Burner Grill Island with storage cabinets and fold-out prep table or the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island Grill, bundles storage, prep space, and sometimes a side burner into one purchase. Island stations cost less than sourcing each component separately but limit your design choices. The trade-off is convenience and speed of setup versus full customization.

What BTU output should I look for in a heavy-duty outdoor gas grill?

For everyday grilling, roughly 10,000 BTU per burner is a reasonable baseline. The grills reviewed across this silo range from 34,000 BTU across two burners on the Stanbroil Built-In Griddle to 48,000 BTU on the Tytus Fresno main burners plus a 12,000 BTU side burner. Higher BTU matters most for searing and recovering temperature quickly after you open the lid. However, total BTU is only meaningful in context — a well-insulated cooking chamber with tightly fitting grates will outperform a higher-BTU grill with poor heat retention. Factor in cooking area alongside BTU when comparing models.

Which subcategory pages should I check based on how I plan to use the grill?

The right starting point depends on your setup. If you are building or upgrading a full outdoor kitchen, Best Gas Grills For Outdoor Kitchen covers built-in and island-compatible options in depth. For a broader look at standalone and semi-portable natural gas units, Best Outdoor Grills Natural Gas is the most comprehensive overview. Buyers focused specifically on backyard barbecue performance should see Best Barbeque Grills Natural Gas or Best Barbecue Grills Natural Gas. If compatibility with an existing natural gas hookup is the primary concern, Best Grills For Natural Gas filters by that criterion directly.

Are stainless steel grills actually more durable outdoors, and which grade matters?

Stainless steel resists rust and surface corrosion far better than painted steel in outdoor environments, but the grade determines how well it holds up over years of use. Grade 304 stainless — used in the Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head — contains higher nickel and chromium content than the more common 430 grade, making it more resistant to pitting in humid or coastal environments. For a grill that will stay permanently installed and exposed to weather year-round, 304 stainless is worth the premium. For covered or seasonal use, 430 stainless performs adequately at a lower cost.

Buying guide and further reading

Porcelain-coated grates, like those on the Tytus Fresno, hold heat evenly and clean up faster but can chip if handled roughly. For a permanent outdoor kitchen drop-in, 304 stainless grates are the safer long-term investment.

Frequently asked questions

What is the minimum budget for a quality natural gas outdoor grill?

Expect to spend at least $500 for a full-featured freestanding natural gas grill. The Monument Grills Larger 4+2 Burner model comes in at $519 and includes infrared side sear burners, a Clearview lid, and cabinet-style storage — making it a credible entry point. Built-in grill heads for outdoor kitchens start higher: the Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner Built-In Head lists at $889.99, while more premium heads like the Hygrill STD Series 3-Burner Built In Grill run $1,129.99. Full island stations with cabinetry occupy a different tier again, beginning around $799.99 for the Brand-Man 4-Burner Grill Island.

What is the difference between a built-in grill head and a grill island?

A built-in grill head — like the Hygrill STD Series Heavy Duty 3-Burner Built In Grill or the Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner Propane Grill Head — is a drop-in unit designed to be framed into a custom masonry or metal outdoor kitchen structure you supply. A grill island is a self-contained station that arrives with the grill, storage cabinets, and sometimes a fold-out prep table already integrated, like the Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island with Side Burner. Islands are faster to deploy; built-in heads give more design flexibility but require additional construction. If you are building a permanent outdoor kitchen from scratch, see Best Gas Grills For Outdoor Kitchen (2026) for a deeper breakdown.

How many BTUs do I need for outdoor natural gas grilling?

For a standard backyard cook, 30,000–45,000 BTUs across the main burners is sufficient. The Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner Built-In Head delivers 40,000 BTUs; the Brand-Man 4-Burner Grill Island outputs 44,000 BTUs. Larger gatherings or commercial-adjacent use benefit from more headroom — the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island Grill provides 48,000 BTUs on the main burners plus an additional 12,000 BTU side burner. BTU count matters less than burner layout and grate material; high BTUs with poor heat retention still underperform a well-designed lower-BTU system.

Can propane grills be converted to natural gas?

Many models on this list are dual-fuel convertible. The Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner Grill Head is explicitly listed as "Natural Gas Convertible," and the Brand-Man 4-Burner Grill Island is sold as "LPG/NG Convertible." The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island Grill also supports both propane and natural gas. Conversion typically requires a manufacturer-supplied orifice kit and a dedicated natural gas line — it is not a reversible field mod on most models. Always verify the conversion kit is included or available before purchasing if you plan to run natural gas.

What cooking surface size should I look for?

For a family of four with occasional entertaining, 500–700 square inches of total cooking surface covers most situations. The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island Grill tops this list at 864 sq. in., making it the clear choice for larger groups. Built-in heads are sized by width: the Hygrill STD Series is a 3-burner head, while the Brand-Man 4-Burner head is 30 inches across. If flat-top cooking is the priority rather than grate cooking, the Stanbroil 28-Inch Premium Built-In Natural Gas Griddle provides 34,000 BTUs across two burners on a dedicated flat surface — a fundamentally different cooking experience suited to smash burgers, breakfast, and stir-fry applications.

Are natural gas outdoor grills worth the cost over propane?

Natural gas grills cost more upfront — both the appliance and the line installation — but the per-BTU fuel cost is consistently lower than propane in most regions, and the convenience of an uninterrupted supply removes the need to swap tanks mid-cook. For permanent outdoor kitchens where the grill will see regular use year-round, the math typically favors natural gas within one to two seasons. For occasional use or a portable setup, propane remains the more practical choice. Browse the full Natural Gas Outdoor Grills hub for additional guidance on setup costs and line requirements.

A full grill station like the Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island includes storage cabinets, a fold-out prep table, and side burner functionality in one 44,000 BTU package at $799.99, which makes more sense when a turnkey setup is the priority. If natural gas is the fuel source, also confirm whether a grill ships NG-ready or requires a conversion kit — some models, like the Brand-Man Built-In Head, list "natural gas convertible," meaning the conversion hardware may be sold separately.

For deeper coverage of how these grills fit into larger outdoor cooking setups, the best gas grills for outdoor kitchen guide covers built-in compatibility and island planning in detail. If you're still comparing natural gas options across a wider field, the best outdoor grills natural gas roundup and the broader natural gas outdoor grills hub are useful next stops.

Frequently asked questions

What is a realistic budget for a heavy-duty natural gas barbecue grill?

Entry-level freestanding grill stations with four burners and storage cabinets start around $800 — the Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island sits at $799.99 and delivers 44,000 BTU with stainless steel construction. Serious built-in grill heads for outdoor kitchens begin closer to $890 (Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner Built-In Head at $889.99) and climb to $1,130–$1,400 for larger or fully appointed setups like the Hygrill STD Series at $1,129.99 or the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island at $1,399.99. A $1,000–$1,200 budget covers most built-in heads with 304 stainless steel and respectable BTU output.

What is the difference between a built-in grill head and a grill island?

A built-in grill head — like the Hygrill STD Series or the Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head — is a drop-in unit designed to be framed into a custom outdoor kitchen counter. It ships without a cabinet base. A grill island, like the Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island or the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island, arrives as a self-contained station with its own cabinet, storage, and sometimes a fold-out prep table. Islands suit buyers who want a turn-key setup; built-in heads suit those building a custom stone or stainless kitchen from scratch.

Can these natural gas grills be converted from propane, or vice versa?

Several models on this list are dual-fuel convertible. The Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner Built-In Head is explicitly listed as natural gas convertible from propane, and the Brand-Man 4-Burner Grill Island is LPG/NG convertible. The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner is designed to run on either propane or natural gas. Conversion typically requires a jet/orifice swap — check whether the conversion kit is included before purchasing, as some manufacturers charge separately for it.

How many BTUs do I actually need for a backyard barbecue grill?

For standard grilling on a 3–4 burner unit, 40,000–44,000 BTU across the main burners is sufficient — both Brand-Man built-in models hit that range. Where BTU output matters most is recovery time after opening the lid and searing performance. The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner pushes 48,000 BTU on the main burners plus a 12,000 BTU side burner, which is practical for large gatherings or wok cooking. Raw BTU numbers are less meaningful without knowing cooking surface area; the Tytus covers 864 sq. in., giving more context to why the higher output is warranted.

Is a flat-top griddle a good alternative to a traditional grate grill for natural gas outdoor cooking?

For breakfasts, smash burgers, seafood, and vegetables, a flat-top griddle outperforms grates. The Stanbroil 28-Inch Premium Built-In Natural Gas Griddle offers 34,000 BTU across two burners and a stainless steel lid, and is purpose-built for outdoor kitchen installation. It is not a substitute for charring steaks over open flame, but for cooks who want versatility or primarily cook items that fall through grates, a built-in griddle alongside a grill head is a practical combination. See the best gas grills for outdoor kitchen guide for more built-in pairing options.

Which natural gas barbecue grill is best for a large outdoor kitchen island build?

The Hygrill STD Series Heavy Duty 3-Burner Built-In Grill and the Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner Built-In Head are both designed specifically for drop-in outdoor kitchen installation using 304 stainless steel construction. The Brand-Man's 30-inch footprint and 40,000 BTU output at $889.99 makes it the more budget-conscious choice; the Hygrill at $1,129.99 earns a perfect 5/5 across 41 reviews and suits buyers prioritizing build quality above price. For a broader look at outdoor kitchen-compatible options, the best gas grills for outdoor kitchen page covers additional configurations.

Buying guide and further reading

BTU output sets the floor for what a natural gas grill can actually do. For a standard backyard cook, 40,000–44,000 BTUs across four burners — as seen on the Brand-Man Gas Grill Built-In Head and the Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island — is enough to run high heat across a full grate. Step up to the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner and you get 48,000 BTU main output plus a dedicated 12,000 BTU side burner, which matters when you need a sauce pot or sear station running simultaneously. Raw BTU numbers only tell part of the story, though; how those BTUs are distributed across the cooking surface is equally important. The Stanbroil 28-Inch Premium Built-In Natural Gas Griddle puts 34,000 BTUs under a flat-top surface rather than open grates, concentrating heat in a way that suits smash burgers and breakfast cooks over traditional grilling.

Cooking area and burner count determine how much food you can move at once. The Tytus Fresno's 864 sq. in. of cooking space is the largest in this group and can handle a full brisket alongside ribs without shuffling anything off the grate. The Hygrill STD Series runs three burners, which is a practical minimum for zone cooking — one burner on low for indirect holds, two on high for direct searing — while keeping the built-in footprint compact enough for tighter outdoor kitchen cutouts. For pure griddle work, the Stanbroil's two-burner flat top gives independent zone control over a 28-inch surface, a meaningful advantage when cooking proteins and vegetables at different temperatures side by side.

Material quality and installation type are the two factors most buyers underweight. Built-in grill heads — the Hygrill STD Series, Brand-Man Built-In Head, and Stanbroil griddle among them — use 304 stainless steel in their high-contact components, which resists the moisture and grease cycling that eats through lower-grade steel within a few seasons. Freestanding or semi-portable options like the Tytus Fresno and Brand-Man Grill Island add storage cabinets and prep tables, making them self-contained stations that don't require a masonry surround. If the grill is going into a permanent island, a built-in head with a dedicated natural gas connection is the cleaner long-term choice; if the setup needs to move or evolve, an island station with NG conversion capability gives more flexibility without sacrificing BTU performance.

For buyers building a full outdoor cooking setup, the best gas grills for outdoor kitchens covers built-in compatibility and island integration in greater depth. Those comparing natural gas options across a wider price range will find additional context in the best barbeque grills for natural gas guide, which includes both built-in and freestanding formats side by side.

Frequently asked questions

What is the minimum BTU output worth considering for a natural gas grill?

For most backyards, 40,000 BTUs across four burners is a reasonable floor for serious grilling. The Brand-Man Gas Grill Built-In Head hits exactly that mark at 40,000 BTUs across four burners, while the Tytus Fresno pushes to 48,000 BTUs on its main burners plus a dedicated 12,000 BTU side burner. Output below 30,000 BTUs tends to struggle with searing large cuts or maintaining temperature in cold weather.

Can I convert a propane grill to natural gas?

Some models are designed for it, but not all. The Brand-Man Gas Grill Built-In Head and the Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island are both explicitly listed as LPG/NG convertible, meaning they ship with or support conversion hardware. The Tytus Fresno is also sold as a propane or natural gas unit. Always confirm a conversion kit is included or available before purchasing — attempting to run a propane-only regulator on natural gas is a safety hazard.

What is the price difference between a built-in grill head and a full island station?

Built-in grill heads designed to drop into an existing outdoor kitchen frame typically range from roughly $890 to $1,130 in this category — the Brand-Man Gas Grill Built-In Head at $889.99 and the Hygrill STD Series at $1,129.99 represent that spread. Full island stations that include storage cabinets, side burners, and prep surfaces start around $800 with the Brand-Man 4-Burner Island at $799.99 and climb to $1,399.99 for the Tytus Fresno with its 864 sq. in. cooking area and semi-portable frame. Budget for installation fittings and a gas line connection on top of either type.

Is a flat-top griddle a practical alternative to a traditional grated natural gas grill?

It depends on what you cook. The Stanbroil 28-Inch Premium Built-In Natural Gas Griddle delivers 34,000 BTUs across two burners on a flat stainless steel surface, which excels at smash burgers, breakfast foods, stir-fries, and anything that would fall through grates. It lacks the char marks and fat-drip flavor of an open-grate grill, so many buyers install both in the same outdoor kitchen island. The Stanbroil includes a lid, making it more weather-resistant than an open griddle.

How many burners do I need for a family of four versus larger gatherings?

Three burners handle everyday family cooking with room for two-zone heat management — the Hygrill STD Series 3-Burner covers that use case. Four burners give more flexibility for simultaneous direct and indirect cooking and are worth the step up if you regularly cook for six or more people. Five burners, like the Tytus Fresno, make sense for frequent entertaining where you need a full sear zone, a low-and-slow zone, and a side burner running simultaneously without juggling cook times.

Do natural gas grills work with standard residential gas lines?

Most residential natural gas lines run at low pressure, typically around 7 inches of water column, which is what consumer-grade natural gas grills are calibrated for. All the natural gas models listed here are designed for standard residential supply. The key requirement is that your home already has a gas line run to the outdoor cooking area — if not, a licensed plumber or gas fitter must install one. Always check local code requirements before connecting any grill to a fixed gas supply.

At 34,000 BTUs across two burners and a dedicated natural gas configuration, it performs as a permanent built-in appliance alongside a conventional grill rather than replacing one. Its 4.5/5 from 20 reviews is solid for a specialized category product.

At $1,109.99 it's priced like a premium grill head despite being a two-burner griddle — that's a meaningful cost for buyers who see it as a secondary outdoor kitchen appliance. It also isn't a direct substitute for a grill; anyone who wants traditional grate cooking needs a separate unit. Four main burners are complemented by two infrared side sear burners — a meaningful differentiator that lets cooks hit steakhouse-level surface temperatures without upgrading to a more expensive unit. Buying guide and further reading

BTU output is the first number worth scrutinizing, but raw heat means little without context. A 3-burner built-in like the Hygrill STD Series sits at the lower burner count, yet its stainless steel construction and purpose-built grill head design concentrate heat efficiently across the cooking surface. Compare that with the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner, which throws 48,000 BTU from the main burners plus a dedicated 12,000 BTU side burner — useful if you're running a full outdoor kitchen station and need simultaneous cooking zones. For most residential patios, 40,000–48,000 BTU across four or five burners is more than sufficient; chasing higher numbers without the cooking area to match them is money wasted.

Construction grade separates grills that last a season from those that last a decade. Brushed 304 stainless steel is the benchmark — it resists salt air, grease, and thermal cycling far better than 430-grade alternatives. The Brand-Man Gas Grill Built-In Head explicitly uses 304 stainless at $889.99, and the Stanbroil 28-Inch Premium Built-In Natural Gas Griddle calls out durable stainless steel at $1,109.99. Porcelain-coated cast-iron grates, as found on the Tytus Fresno, retain heat well and are forgiving to clean, but they require more care to prevent chipping than solid stainless grates.

OUR TOP PICK

The Hygrill STD Series Heavy Duty 3-Burner Built In Grill, Stainless Steel Grill Head for Outdoor Kitchen, Natural Gas nails heavy-duty stainless steel durability, a dedicated built-in head design sized for outdoor kitchen drop-in installation, and a perfect 5/5 rating across 41 verified buyers. $1129.99

VIEW ON AMAZON →

Convertibility and installation format are the third axis. A built-in grill head — like the Hygrill or the Brand-Man 30-inch 4-burner — drops into a masonry or steel island and stays put. A station-style unit such as the Brand-Man 4-Burner Grill Island ships with storage cabinets, a fold-out prep table, and LPG/NG conversion hardware already in the box, which lowers the total project cost if you're building from scratch. Semi-portable designs like the Tytus Fresno occupy a middle ground, offering storage cabinets and 864 sq. in. of cooking area without requiring permanent installation. Match the format to how finished — and how fixed — your outdoor cooking setup is before committing.

For shoppers integrating one of these grills into a permanent setup, the best gas grills for outdoor kitchen covers drop-in compatibility, island clearances, and ventilation requirements in detail. If you want a broader look across the full category before narrowing to barbecue-style units, the natural gas outdoor grills hub pulls together top picks, comparison guides, and buying advice across every configuration — from freestanding carts to full built-in heads.

Frequently asked questions

What is the price floor for a quality natural gas barbecue grill?

Expect to spend at least $500 for a grill that combines durable construction with enough BTU output for serious cooking. The Monument Grills Larger 4+2 Burner at $519 sits at the lower end of that range while still offering 304 stainless steel construction, infrared side sear burners, and a Clearview lid. Built-in grill heads and full island stations with storage typically start around $800–$900, as seen with the Brand-Man 4-burner options, and climb past $1,100 for premium configurations like the Hygrill STD Series or the Stanbroil 28-Inch Griddle.

What is the difference between a built-in grill head and a freestanding island grill?

A built-in grill head — like the Hygrill STD Series 3-Burner or the Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner — is a grill body designed to drop into a custom outdoor kitchen counter or masonry frame you already have or plan to build. A freestanding island grill, such as the Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island or the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner, arrives as a complete station with its own cabinet frame, storage, and prep surfaces. If you are building an outdoor kitchen from scratch, a built-in head gives more design flexibility. If you want a ready-to-use setup, a full island unit is the faster path.

Can propane grills listed here run on natural gas?

Several models on this list are explicitly convertible. The Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner is sold as "Natural Gas Convertible," and the Brand-Man 4-Burner Island Station is listed as "LPG/NG Convertible." The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner also supports both propane and natural gas. Conversion typically requires a separate orifice kit or a factory-supplied conversion kit — confirm what is included before purchase, and always have a qualified technician handle the gas line connection.

How much cooking space do these grills offer?

The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner leads the group with 864 sq. in. of total cooking area alongside a 12,000 BTU side burner. The Stanbroil 28-Inch Built-In Griddle trades traditional grates for a flat top surface suited to smash burgers, seafood, and breakfast items. Built-in heads like the Hygrill 3-Burner and Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner are sized for outdoor kitchen counter integration, so their primary cooking areas are more compact by design. For families or frequent large-format cooks, the 5-burner or 4+2 burner configurations deliver the most versatility.

Is a natural gas grill better than propane for permanent outdoor kitchen installations?

Natural gas is generally the better choice for a permanent installation. It connects directly to your home's gas line, so there are no propane tanks to swap, no running out mid-cook, and no storage space required. Operating costs per BTU tend to be lower than propane in most regions. The trade-off is that installation requires a licensed plumber or gas technician to run the line, which adds upfront cost. For a built-in unit you plan to use year-round, that one-time expense is typically worth it.

Which grill here makes the most sense for an outdoor kitchen island build?

The Hygrill STD Series Heavy Duty 3-Burner Built-In Grill at $1,129.99 and the Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner Built-In Head at $889.99 are the two purpose-built drop-in options. The Brand-Man delivers 40,000 BTUs across four burners and carries a perfect 5/5 rating across 72 reviews, making it a strong value pick. The Hygrill suits builders who want a heavy-duty stainless head with a simpler 3-burner layout. For a complete ready-made outdoor kitchen without custom construction, the Brand-Man 4-Burner Island Station at $799.99 includes storage cabinets and a fold-out prep table out of the box. More outdoor kitchen-specific options are covered in the Best Gas Grills For Outdoor Kitchen (2026) guide.

Buying guide and further reading

BTU output sets the floor for what a natural gas barbecue grill can actually do. The products here range from 34,000 BTUs on the Stanbroil 28-Inch Premium Built-In Natural Gas Griddle up to 60,000 BTUs combined (48,000 main plus 12,000 side burner) on the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island Grill. More BTUs matter most when you're cooking for large groups or need rapid heat recovery after loading a cold brisket — but raw output means little without even distribution across the cooking surface. Check burner count alongside total BTUs: spreading 44,000 BTUs across four burners on the Brand-Man 4-Burner Built-In Head delivers more zone-control flexibility than the same output crammed into two burners.

Cooking surface area and grate material directly affect versatility. The Tytus Fresno offers 864 sq. in. of total cooking area with porcelain-coated grates, which hold heat evenly and resist sticking better than bare cast iron at the cost of some durability if chipped. Stainless steel grates, found on most built-in heads here, are easier to maintain and won't rust if left outdoors, which matters more for a permanent outdoor kitchen installation than for a wheeled cart. For flat-top cooking — smash burgers, seafood, breakfast foods — the Stanbroil griddle with its 28-inch stainless steel flat top is a fundamentally different tool than a grated grill, and worth considering as a second station rather than a direct substitute.

Built-in heads versus freestanding island grills is the final fork in the decision. A drop-in grill head like the Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner or the Hygrill STD Series integrates cleanly into a masonry or steel outdoor kitchen frame you already own or plan to build, keeping the footprint tight. Complete island stations — like the Brand-Man 4-Burner Grill Island with fold-out prep table and storage cabinets, or the semi-portable Tytus Fresno — bundle the enclosure into the purchase at a lower combined cost, but limit your layout flexibility. Budget buyers who want a complete freestanding setup at $519 should also look at the Monument Grills 4+2 Burner, which adds infrared side sear burners and a clearview lid at a fraction of the built-in price.

Frequently asked questions

What is the minimum budget for a quality natural gas barbecue grill?

Expect to spend at least $500 for a freestanding natural gas grill with meaningful BTU output and stainless steel construction. The Monument Grills 4+2 Burner at $519 sits at that entry point and includes infrared side sear burners and a clearview lid. Built-in grill heads for outdoor kitchens start closer to $890, as seen with the Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner head at $889.99. Full island stations with storage cabinets and prep tables range from $800 to $1,400 depending on burner count and cook surface.

Can propane grills on this list be converted to natural gas?

Several models are explicitly dual-fuel capable. The Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner Built-In Head is listed as natural gas convertible, and the Brand-Man 4-Burner Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island is rated LPG/NG convertible. The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island Grill is sold as propane or natural gas. Conversion kits are typically included or available from the manufacturer — confirm before purchase that the kit ships with the unit or is sold separately, since running a propane-only orifice on natural gas will starve the burners of fuel.

How much cooking area do these grills actually offer?

Cooking area varies significantly across this lineup. The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island Grill leads with 864 sq. in. of total cooking space and five burners producing 48,000 BTU on the main grates plus a 12,000 BTU side burner. The Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner head delivers 40,000 BTU across four burners in a more compact built-in footprint. The Stanbroil 28-Inch Built-In Griddle uses a flat-top format with 34,000 BTU across two burners — better suited to smash burgers and breakfast cooks than traditional grate grilling. Match the cooking area to your typical batch size: a 30-inch grill head handles a family cookout, while a 48-inch-class island handles catering-scale loads.

What is the difference between a built-in grill head and a freestanding island grill?

A built-in grill head — like the Hygrill STD Series 3-Burner or the Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner — is designed to drop into a custom outdoor kitchen counter or masonry surround. It ships without cabinetry, so you need an existing or contractor-built island. A freestanding island grill, such as the Brand-Man 4-Burner Grill Island or the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner, arrives as a complete station with storage cabinets and prep surfaces included. If you are building an outdoor kitchen from scratch and want a custom look, a built-in head offers more design flexibility. If you want a ready-to-use setup without construction, a full island is the faster path.

Is 304 stainless steel important, and which grills use it?

Yes. 304 stainless steel contains 18% chromium and 8% nickel, making it substantially more corrosion-resistant than lower-grade 430 stainless — a real concern when a grill is permanently plumbed to a natural gas line and left outdoors year-round. The Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner Built-In Head explicitly specifies 304 stainless steel construction. The Hygrill STD Series also uses stainless steel construction for its grill head. When a listing does not specify the grade, ask the seller before buying, particularly for coastal or high-humidity climates.

Do any of these grills include a side burner for sauces or sides?

Yes. The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island Grill includes a dedicated 12,000 BTU side burner alongside its 48,000 BTU main burner array — enough output for a large stockpot or a cast-iron sear pan. The Monument Grills 4+2 Burner also includes side burners as part of its configuration. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island adds a side burner to its full station setup. Built-in heads like the Hygrill and Brand-Man 30-Inch head do not include a side burner by nature of their drop-in format, though a separate side burner module can often be added to an outdoor kitchen island during construction.

Frequently asked questions

What is the minimum budget for a quality natural gas BBQ grill?

Expect to spend at least $500 for a grill with meaningful build quality and cooking capacity. The Monument Grills Larger 4+2 Burner Propane Gas Grill comes in at $519 and offers stainless steel cabinet construction, infrared side sear burners, and a clearview lid — making it the lowest realistic entry point among heavy-duty options. Below that price range, materials and BTU output tend to drop off sharply.

Can natural gas grills be converted to propane, or vice versa?

Some models ship with conversion kits or are explicitly rated for both fuels. The Brand-Man Gas Grill Built-In Head (30-inch, 4-burner) is labeled "Natural Gas Convertible," and the Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island is marked "LPG/NG Convertible." The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island is also rated for either propane or natural gas. Always confirm a kit is included or available before purchasing — not every stainless steel grill head supports dual-fuel operation without additional parts.

What's the difference between a built-in grill head and a freestanding grill station?

A built-in grill head — like the Hygrill STD Series 3-Burner or the Stanbroil 28-Inch Natural Gas Griddle — is designed to drop into a custom outdoor kitchen frame or BBQ island you already own or plan to build. A freestanding grill station, such as the Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island or the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island, includes its own cabinet base, storage, and prep surfaces, so no separate island construction is needed. If you're starting from scratch, a station is faster and often more cost-effective; if you're finishing a masonry or steel island, a drop-in head gives a cleaner look.

How much cooking area do these natural gas grills offer?

Cooking surface varies significantly by model. The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island tops the group at 864 sq. in. of total cooking area. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Island and the Monument Grills 4+2 Burner unit are mid-range freestanding options suited for groups of six to ten. The Stanbroil 28-Inch Griddle is the most compact at 28 inches wide — better for households that prefer flat-top cooking over grate-style grilling. Match the cooking area to your typical party size: roughly 72 sq. in. per person is a workable rule of thumb.

Which of these grills delivers the highest BTU output?

The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner Island leads with 48,000 BTU from the main burners plus a 12,000 BTU side burner, totaling 60,000 BTU. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Built-In Head puts out 40,000 BTU, and the Brand-Man 4-Burner Island station reaches 44,000 BTU. The Stanbroil flat-top griddle delivers 34,000 BTU across two burners. Higher BTU matters most for searing and cold-weather grilling; for everyday cooking, heat distribution and grate quality often matter more than raw output alone.

Are 304 stainless steel grills worth the premium over standard stainless?

Yes, for outdoor use. Grade 304 stainless contains a higher nickel and chromium content than lower-grade alloys, giving it substantially better resistance to moisture, salt air, and the thermal cycling that outdoor grills endure. The Brand-Man Gas Grill Built-In Head explicitly specifies 304 stainless steel construction. If a product listing does not state the grade, assume it is a lesser alloy — relevant for buyers in coastal or high-humidity climates where corrosion accelerates quickly. Frequently asked questions

What is the minimum budget for a quality natural gas grill?

Expect to spend at least $500 for a freestanding cabinet-style gas grill with meaningful BTU output. The Monument Grills Larger 4+2 Burner comes in at $519 and represents a realistic floor for a full-featured unit with infrared sear burners and a clearview lid. Built-in grill heads for outdoor kitchens start higher — the Brand-Man 30-Inch 4-Burner Built-In Head runs $889.99, and premium 304 stainless options like the Hygrill STD Series 3-Burner climb to $1,129.99.

Can I convert a propane grill to natural gas?

Several grills on this list are factory-convertible. The Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island ($799.99) and the Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head ($889.99) are both listed as LPG/NG convertible, meaning conversion hardware is supported by the manufacturer. The Tytus Fresno 5-Burner is also available in a propane or natural gas configuration. Always use a manufacturer-supplied conversion kit — aftermarket kits can void warranties and create safety hazards.

What is the difference between a built-in grill head and a freestanding grill island?

A built-in grill head — like the Hygrill STD Series or Stanbroil 28-Inch Natural Gas Griddle — is a cooking unit designed to drop into a countertop cutout in an existing outdoor kitchen structure. A freestanding grill island, such as the Brand-Man 4-Burner Gas Outdoor Kitchen Grill Island or the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner, includes its own cabinet frame, storage, and sometimes a fold-out prep table, so no separate structure is needed. Built-ins offer a cleaner look in a custom setup; islands are faster to deploy and relocate. For more guidance on integrating either type, see Best Gas Grills For Outdoor Kitchen (2026).

How many BTUs do I actually need for a natural gas grill?

BTU output should be evaluated against cooking surface area, not in isolation. The Stanbroil 28-Inch Natural Gas Griddle delivers 34,000 BTUs across a flat-top surface, while the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner puts out 48,000 BTUs main burner heat plus a 12,000 BTU side burner across 864 sq. in. of cooking area. For most residential use — grilling for four to eight people — 40,000 to 50,000 combined BTUs is sufficient. Chasing higher BTU numbers without a proportionally large cooking surface typically means wasted fuel rather than better results.

Is 304 stainless steel worth the premium over standard stainless?

Yes, particularly for built-in or permanent outdoor installations. Grade 304 stainless contains a higher chromium and nickel content than 430 stainless, making it significantly more resistant to rust and surface pitting from moisture and cooking grease. The Brand-Man 30-Inch Built-In Head and Hygrill STD Series both specify 304 stainless construction. For a grill that sits exposed year-round on a gas line, the corrosion resistance extends service life meaningfully and reduces long-term maintenance.

Do natural gas grills require professional installation?

Connecting any grill to a permanent natural gas line requires a licensed plumber or gas technician in most jurisdictions — this is not a DIY task. The grill itself may be straightforward to assemble, but the gas line hookup, shutoff valve placement, and pressure testing must meet local code. Freestanding grill islands with their own frames, like the Tytus Fresno or Brand-Man 4-Burner Island, still need a certified connection at the gas supply point. Budget $100–$300 for a professional hookup when pricing out your total project cost.

Buying guide and further reading

BTU output sets the ceiling on how fast a grill recovers heat after the lid opens — and the gap between products here is significant. The Monument Grills 4+2 Burner starts at the lower end of the lineup, while the Tytus Fresno 5-Burner pushes 48,000 BTU on the main burners alone plus a 12,000 BTU side burner. For everyday family cooking, anything in the 34,000–44,000 BTU range (like the Brand-Man 30-inch head at 40,000 BTU or the Brand-Man Island Station at 44,000 BTU) hits the sweet spot between fuel efficiency and searing power. Chasing maximum BTU without checking cooking area is a common mistake — the Tytus backs its output with 864 sq. in. of cooking surface, which justifies the higher output.

Burner count and grill format — built-in head versus freestanding island — should match how your outdoor space is set up. Built-in heads like the Hygrill STD Series 3-Burner or the Stanbroil 28-Inch Griddle drop into a custom enclosure and leave cabinetry to you. Freestanding island units like the Brand-Man Island Station ship with storage cabinets and a fold-out prep table, which is a meaningful value-add if you're not ready to pour a full outdoor kitchen. The Tytus Fresno is semi-portable, a rare middle ground for buyers who want island-style cooking without a permanent installation.

OUR TOP PICK
